channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jeff Bezos' space company calls off debut launch of massive new rocket in final minutes of countdown
item.description: Blue Origin called off the debut launch of its massive new rocket early Monday because of technical trouble.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Incoming Trump team is questioning civil servants at National Security Council about their loyalty
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Incoming senior Trump administration officials have begun questioning career civil servants who work on the White House National Security Council about who they voted for in the 2024 election, their political contributions and whether they have made social media posts that could be
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New Jersey mayor floats 'reverse congestion pricing' toll in response to NYC's controversial toll
item.description: A New Jersey mayor is proposing reverse congestion pricing toll after New York City recently implemented its controversial congestion pricing toll.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jayden Daniels leads Commanders to first playoff win in nearly 7,000 days, 23-20 over the Buccaneers
item.description:
New owners. New general manager. New coach. New franchise quarterback.
That’s the formula — with an emphasis on the QB — that Washington rode to playoff success after nearly two decades of futility.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden says he was the steady hand the world needed after Trump, who's ready to shake things up again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden strode into the White House four years ago with a foreign policy agenda that put repairing alliances strained by four years of Republican Donald Trump's “America First” worldview front and center.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Lamar Jackson and Josh Allen will face off for a berth in the AFC championship game
item.description: Lamar Jackson vs. Josh Allen won’t decide the NFL MVP next week. The stakes are higher with a spot in the AFC championship game on the line.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires ripping through the Los Angeles area have killed at least 24 people, displaced thousands of others and destroyed more than 12,000 structures while burning through an area larger than the city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fencing, roof of Anchorage pedestrian bridge fall in strong wind, closing highway
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The side fencing and roof of a pedestrian bridge fell in strong wind early Sunday, closing the main highway south of Anchorage in both directions until the debris could be cleared.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Biden speaks with relatives of Americans held by Taliban, but deal to bring them home still elusive
item.description: President Joe Biden has spoken with relatives of three Americans the U.S. government is looking to bring home from Afghanistan, but no agreement has been reached on a deal to get them back.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fires burn Los Angeles schools and destroy outdoor education sanctuaries
item.description: For Irina Contreras, a program manager for Los Angeles County’s Department of Arts and Culture, outdoor education was a refuge for both her and her daughter during the pandemic. Now, much of that refuge has been burned in the raging wildfires around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: CHEYANNE MUMPHREY and SHARON LURYE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Southern areas hit by winter storm thaw and power slowly returns
item.description: After a freezing winter storm shut schools, cut power and cancelled or delayed flights, the South was slowly thawing Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance says those who ‘committed violence’ on Jan. 6 ‘obviously’ shouldn’t be pardoned
item.description: (The Hill) -- Vice President-elect JD Vance said people who “committed violence” on Jan. 6 “obviously” shouldn’t be pardoned by President-elect Trump. “I think it’s very simple.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reality TV star Chase Chrisley accused of slapping Twin Peaks bar manager in Atlanta
item.description: Chrisley, who gained fame on the “Chrisley Knows Best” show that featured his parents and close-knit family, was allegedly drunk at a Twin Peaks restaurant on Jan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles tree advocates hope their vital work recovers from devastating fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Some people across Los Angeles have worked for years to increase the number of trees that give respite from heat and air pollution. The tree advocates have confronted increasing drought, bad trimming and objections from neighbors who resent leaves and sap.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LAPD responds to Kamala Harris' California home in Palisades Fire evacuation zone, 2 men detained: reports
item.description: The LAPD reportedly detained two men near Kamala Harris' Brentwood home in the Palisades Fire evacuation zone after potential burglary call came in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: 16 dead, more than a dozen missing as crews continue fighting blazes
item.description: More than a dozen people have died as a result of the still-spreading wildfires burning in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, ABC 4

item.title: California relying on prisoners, Mexico to provide firefighters to battle Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: California has deployed firefighters from Mexico as well as nearly 1,000 prison inmates to help combat deadly wildfires that have exploded across Los Angeles since last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New England Patriots hire former Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as next coach
item.description: Vrabel, a fan favorite during eight seasons as a player in New England where he was a member of its first three Super Bowl winners and an inductee into the team’s Hall of Fame in 2023, was the preferred candidate because of his long association with the franchise and coaching success.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 24 dead as fire crews try to corral Los Angeles blazes before winds return this week
item.description: Firefighters scrambled to make further progress against wildfires that have killed at least 24 people in the Los Angeles area as forecasters again warned of dangerous weather with the return of strong winds this week.
item.dc:creator: Jae Hong, Holly Ramer And Michael R. Blood, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Walmart recalls 12,000 cartons of chicken broth
item.description: Walmart has recalled over 12,000 cartons of Great Value Family Size Chicken Broth due to packaging issues that could lead to spoilage, according to the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA fire chief says city failed department amid ongoing Southern California wildfires
item.description: Los Angeles Fire Department (LAFD) Chief Kristin Crowley said during an interview Friday that the city failed the department amid the ongoing wildfires in Southern California that have taken at least 11 lives and burned more than 37,000 acres.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Are there no Michelin-starred restaurants near you? There's a reason for that
item.description: You'd have difficulty finding Michelin-starred restaurants in most states — but it's not because there aren't excellent eaters in those places.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: How a week of weather extremes upended the lives of millions of Americans
item.description: It was a week of fire and ice. It began with millions of people across the U.S. shivering amid blizzard conditions and frigid air that lasted for days, thanks to a jet stream that slips out of its usual path more often these days.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LA wildfires destroy numerous houses of worship. Clergy and congregants vow to persevere
item.description: Flames were already attacking the campus of Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center when the cantor, Ruth Berman Harris, and three companions rushed in to rescue its sacred Torah scrolls.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden takes unexpected, impromptu questions from press in final days
item.description: President Biden opted Friday to voluntarily take a host of questions from the press after delivering remarks on the day’s job report, a speech that had been put on the White House schedule last minute.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Texas, Arizona arming Mexican drug cartels
item.description: The U.S. and Mexico are working closer than ever to trace the origin of guns used in drug cartel massacres and other crimes south of the border.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Newsom calls for investigation into water supply after tanks 'went dry' during wildfire
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has ordered an investigation into the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power amid reports of a loss of water pressure to fire hydrants and limited water resources in the wildfire zones.
item.dc:creator: Eytan Wallace, Fox 5

item.title: 18 migrants found stowed in tanker truck near San Antonio
item.description: A group of 18 migrants were found inside a tanker truck by Texas law enforcement hundreds of miles from the border in Guadalupe County, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Palisades Fire threatens Los Angeles' San Fernando Valley
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - The Palisades Fire, the most destructive wildfire in Los Angeles history, edged closer to one of the city's major population centers on Friday: the San Fernando Valley.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, ABC 4

item.title: Lisa Kudrow says Matthew Perry left a note for her inside a recognizable 'Friends' prop
item.description: "I hadn't opened it up or looked inside it, but yeah he did. He had a note in there and I forgot about it," Kudrow said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, ABC 4

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg returns to his wildfire-charred LA neighborhood
item.description: Steve Guttenberg awoke Thursday morning to a grim reality: The treacherous wildfire that tore through the Pacific Palisades had left his once-lush neighborhood charred and unrecognizable.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Civilian drone hits, damages firefighting aircraft over Palisades Fire
item.description: A firefighting aircraft that was operating over the Palisades Fire is grounded and out of service after it was struck and damaged by a civilian’s drone on Thursday afternoon, officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4
